title: pull out unstable release candidate from the fedora stable software repository

comments.body:
We build the RC for all Fedora releases, but the build script should not be
creating a Bodhi for them - they should never hit stable. We’ve been doing
this for over a year without issue. Unfortunately this doesn’t seem to have
worked this time and an RC did somehow slip into stable. Not sure on
specifics yet - we definitely did not push ourselves, so it must have
autopushed, but any negative karma should have halted that?

Regardless, we’re going to do an epoch bump to revert to 3.1.2.

On Tue, May 11, 2021 at 04:54 Andrii Maksymets ***@***.***>
wrote:
